propositions: Removed env vars are fully silent and removed TOML keys only get a generic unknown-key warning, so retired store/security/policy inputs redirect the store or weaken policy without operator signal.
A RemovedInput tombstone registry (kind env/cfg/file, name, replacement, removed_in, behavior) classifies retired inputs as error or warn while unknown keys keep the existing forward-compat policy.

Error-class tombstones raise RemovedInputError subclassing BaseException so it propagates through tracker_dir()'s except ConfigError and MCP boot except Exception handlers instead of being swallowed into a silent fallback.
Env, file-presence, and TOML-key tombstone checks must run at the top of load_config() before the _RESULT_CACHE lookup so they fire on warm cache hits in the long-running MCP server.
A top-level CLI handler in main() wraps all early-return intercepts plus _dispatch() to surface a targeted migration message and nonzero exit rather than a raw traceback.
A CI golden-file check diffs live config keys and env vars against tombstone/alias registries and fails if a key is removed without a corresponding tombstone entry.
